data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T112346-d01e1162/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T112346-d01e1162/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T112346-d01e1162/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T112346-d01e1162/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T112346-d01e1162/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-ae932179">
    <title>repo-structure: extract route-attempt health and liveness routing mechanics</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Root facade files still own route-attempt feedback, liveness escalation, candidate rejection, and effective policy mechanics that ADR-008 assigns to internal implementation packages.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` requires root to stay a public facade while mechanics move behind internal packages. `service.go:18-39` defines `ResolveRoute` and `RecordRouteAttempt` as stable public boundary methods. The implementation remains rooted in `service_routing.go:1` and `service_route_attempts.go:1`, keeping route health state and candidate filtering in the facade.

PROPOSED FIX
Move route-attempt feedback, cooldown/TTL state, liveness escalation, candidate rejection reasons, and effective policy calculation into `internal/routehealth`. Keep root-owned CONTRACT-003 request/response/event types and make `Service.ResolveRoute` and `Service.RecordRouteAttempt` thin adapters around the internal implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not extract provider/model listing surfaces (`ListModels`, `ListProviders`, `HealthCheck`) except for adapter touch needed to keep compilation green. Do not refresh website docs or changelog in this slice beyond incidental references needed by tests.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / DEPENDENCY
Governing artifact: ADR-008 (`spec:ADR-008`). Parent bead/dependency: `fizeau-cd9af16b`.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `service_routing.go` and `service_route_attempts.go` no longer own route-attempt health, cooldown/TTL, liveness escalation, or candidate rejection mechanics; they delegate to `internal/routehealth` while public CONTRACT-003 types remain in the root package.
2. Public behavior and JSON shape for `ResolveRoute`, `RecordRouteAttempt`, route decisions, candidate fields, rejection/filter reasons, effective power policy, role, and correlation ID remain unchanged.
3. Focused root tests pass: `go test . -run 'TestResolveRouteSuccessIncludesCandidates|TestResolveRoutePolicyReportsEffectivePowerPolicy|TestResolveRoute_LivenessEscalation|TestRecordRouteAttempt_.*|TestRoleAndCorrelationID_ResolveRouteParityWithExecute' -count=1`.
4. Internal route-health tests pass: `go test ./internal/routehealth/... -count=1`.
5. Full repository gate passes: `go test ./... -count=1`.
6. Hook gate passes: `lefthook run pre-commit`.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:implementation, area:repo-structure, kind:refactor, spec:ADR-008, area:routing</labels>
    <metadata parent="fizeau-930b498b" base-rev="02a3c54b1d54c737d48c53959b9d0ad691edebf7" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T112346-d01e1162"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
**Result**

Extracted the routing-health mechanics into [internal/routehealth/routing_helpers.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-ae932179-20260515T112346-d01e1162/internal/routehealth/routing_helpers.go) with focused coverage in [internal/routehealth/routing_helpers_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-ae932179-20260515T112346-d01e1162/internal/routehealth/routing_helpers_test.go). The root adapters in [service_routing.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-ae932179-20260515T112346-d01e1162/service_routing.go), [service_route_attempts.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-ae932179-20260515T112346-d01e1162/service_route_attempts.go), and [service_routestatus.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-ae932179-20260515T112346-d01e1162/service_routestatus.go) now delegate effective power policy, route-attempt cooldown projection, dispatchability classification, snapshot cooldown mapping, filter-reason mapping, and policy-ladder escalation while keeping the public CONTRACT-003 types unchanged.

Committed as `9a3c5e7f` with message `refactor: extract route health routing helpers [fizeau-ae932179]`.

**Verification**

Ran:
- `go test . -run 'TestResolveRouteSuccessIncludesCandidates|TestResolveRoutePolicyReportsEffectivePowerPolicy|TestResolveRoute_LivenessEscalation|TestRecordRouteAttempt_.*|TestRoleAndCorrelationID_ResolveRouteParityWithExecute' -count=1`
- `go test ./internal/routehealth/... -count=1`
- `go test ./... -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

I left bead closure to the orchestrator; `ddx bead close` here would have required a post-commit tracker write outside the single-commit execution rule.
